overview: The Village of Weston Plan Commission approved a site plan for the new Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive, and adopted a new salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection areas. Both items passed unanimously with minimal discussion.

agenda.item: Call to order and roll call
Breath and communication disclosure and recusal
Site plan review - Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive
Unfinished business - Creation of salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
April 2026 staff approved certified survey maps and site plans
Planning and development department monthly project update report

discussions.item: Site plan review - Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility
discussions.body: Staff presented the site plan for the new police department and municipal court at 3600 Community Center Drive. The building was rotated to address challenges with a gas pipeline easement running through the property, allowing the parking lot to be placed over the pipeline while keeping the building on higher ground to reduce fill costs. Chief Jeremy explained the building will accommodate 50-60 officers for future growth beyond the current 40 officers. Discussion included an alternate storage shed location and future expansion possibilities to the north. A motion to recommend approval to the board of trustees with staff conditions 2 through 5 passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
discussions.body: Staff proposed a new inspection program requiring two annual inspections per facility - a warm weather inspection focusing on structural integrity and site drainage, and a winter operational inspection focusing on material handling and containment. Jennifer presented a standardized inspection worksheet similar to DOT standards. Staff noted one existing facility near the village well on Sternberg has never gone through the wellhead protection conditional use process and should be contacted. Commissioner Lumper praised the program as well put together. Motion to approve passed unanimously with addition that staff report back to both Plan Commission and Public Works committee.
Motion to approve minutes as presented passed unanimously with no discussion.

discussions.item: April 2026 staff approved certified survey maps and site plans
Planning and development department monthly project update
discussions.body: Jennifer announced the first meeting of the joint Plan Commission and Village Board steering committee for the comprehensive plan update is scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 5:00 p.m. Staff will send email confirmation and calendar invites.

publicComment: Jim Pensson of 5002 Arrow Street, Weston, spoke via remote connection. He urged the commission to exercise common sense regarding landscaping requirements for the new police department to avoid unnecessary taxpayer expense, similar to accommodations made for the Greenheck Turner building. He also requested the commission require annual reporting on the salt shed inspection program to track compliance over time.
actionItems: Site plan for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive recommended for approval to Board of Trustees
Salt storage shed inspection program adopted with twice-annual inspections beginning summer 2026
Staff to report inspection findings to Plan Commission and Public Works committee around May 2027
Staff to contact existing salt storage facility near Sternberg well about wellhead protection compliance
Joint Plan Commission and Village Board steering committee meeting scheduled for June 1st at 5:00 p.m.
Staff to send comprehensive plan steering committee meeting details via email
Electric vehicle charging station regulations to be addressed in fall 2026
Staff to share Ehlers presentation on residential TIF districts with commission
title: Plan Commission
